page_content:
HESP489 Undergraduate Research Experience (1-3 Credits) Undergraduate research experience working under HESP faculty or outside afﬁliates. Prerequisite: HESP202. Restriction: Permission of BSOS-Hearing & Speech Sciences department; and sophomore standing or higher. Repeatable to: 6 credits. Formerly: HESP388.

HESP498 Seminar (3 Credits) Selected topics in human communication and its disorders. Restriction: Permission of BSOS-Hearing & Speech Sciences department. Repeatable to: 6 credits if content differs.

HESP605 Assessment & Intervention in Bilingual Populations (3 Credits) Integrates foundational knowledge pertaining to bilingualism in speech- language pathology. This course provides students with a framework for working with individuals from culturally and linguistically diverse backgrounds. This course is designed to educate and train student clinicians to serve as bilingual speech-language pathologists. Restriction: Must be in Hearing and Speech Sciences: M.A. (Master's) program.
